title: Phase 1 — Text UI Prototype
problem: The engine is proven (94 tests green) but there is no playable game yet. We need a fully playable loop — grid exploration, combat, loot, and inventory — using only text labels and colored boxes (no pixel art, no Skia). Fun must be validated on a real device before any art work begins.

lineage.spawnReason: scope split: Phase 1 text UI prototype — playable 5-floor run with colored View grid, D-pad controls, combat transaction screen, flat-list inventory

analysis: All engine systems are proven (94 tests, 0 failures). Phase 1 connects them to a minimal React Native UI. Grid: 50×50 rendered as colored View cells in a viewport (no Skia yet — ScrollView with absolute positioning). Combat: a single screen showing monster name+affixes, HP bars as View widths, and three buttons (Attack/Potion/Flee). Inventory: flat ScrollView list of Item cards with Equip button. Navigation: simple React state machine (screen enum), no react-navigation overhead at this stage. HUD overlays stamina bar + HP + floor depth on the grid screen. Goal: validate the core fun loop on a real device before committing to pixel art.
implementation: 1. STORES: Zustand slices — gridStore (floor generation, fog, player pos, stamina), combatStore (active monster, round log, player HP), inventoryStore (flat list + equipped slots), gameStore (floor depth, seed, run state). 2. GRID SCREEN: FlatList of rows, each row is a FlatList of cells (View 16×16px). Tile colors: Wall=black, Floor=dark gray, Exit=green, Fog=near-black. Player dot = white circle. D-pad: 4 arrow buttons fixed at bottom-left. Swipe detection via PanResponder. 3. COMBAT SCREEN: Modal overlay. Monster display name + affix badges as colored Text. HP bars: View with width% style. Round log: last 3 lines as Text. Three buttons: Attack (red), Potion (green, disabled if 0), Flee (gray). 4. LOOT SCREEN: After combat victory, show dropped items with quality color. Tap to pick up (auto-place in inventory) or Leave. 5. INVENTORY SCREEN: ScrollView of ItemCard rows. Each card shows name (quality color), size, sockets, stats. Equip button swaps with current slot item. 6. HUD: absolute positioned overlay on GridScreen — stamina bar top-left, HP top-right, floor number top-center.

decisions.decision: React state machine (screen enum) instead of react-navigation for Phase 1
decisions.rationale: react-navigation adds ~2MB and requires native linking. For a prototype validating fun, a simple currentScreen state switch is faster to implement and debug. Can migrate to react-navigation in Phase 3 when screens multiply.
alternativesConsidered: react-navigation — correct long-term but heavy for a prototype
expo-router — even heavier, file-based routing overkill for 4 screens
decisions.recommendation: useGameScreen() hook returning current screen + setter. Stack-agnostic concept.

decisions.decision: Grid rendered as View cells (not Skia) in Phase 1
decisions.rationale: Skia adds native build complexity. For a text prototype, RN View cells are sufficient to validate movement feel and encounter triggers. Skia canvas is Phase 5 (polish). The engine grid logic is completely decoupled from rendering so the swap is a UI-only change.
alternativesConsidered: Skia from day 1 — correct for production but slows down prototype iteration
decisions.recommendation: 16×16 View cells in a FlatList. Viewport: 15 cols × 20 rows visible. Camera pans by offsetting the visible window from player position.

successCriteria:
- Player can move on the grid with D-pad and swipe
- Fog of war reveals as player explores
- Stepping on nodes triggers encounters (combat, chest, shrine)
- Combat screen shows monster name, affixes, HP bars, and 3 action buttons
- Fight resolves round by round with visible damage numbers
- After victory, loot screen shows colored item names to pick up
- Inventory screen lists all items with equip button
- HUD shows HP, stamina, floor depth at all times
- Player can descend to next floor via exit tile
- Full 5-floor run is completable without crashes
knownLimitations: No pixel art — colored boxes only
No Skia canvas — performance may suffer at 50×50 with many View cells (acceptable for prototype)
Inventory is flat list, not Tetris grid (Phase 3)
componentFile: App.tsx, src/store/gridStore.ts, src/store/combatStore.ts, src/store/inventoryStore.ts, src/store/gameStore.ts, src/screens/GridScreen.tsx, src/screens/CombatScreen.tsx, src/screens/LootScreen.tsx, src/screens/InventoryScreen.tsx, src/components/DPad.tsx, src/components/HUD.tsx, src/components/ItemCard.tsx
userGuide:
## Dev Testing
1. `expo start` — scan QR with Expo Go on iOS
2. Move with D-pad or swipe on the grid
3. Encounter triggers automatically on unvisited tiles
4. In combat: tap Attack repeatedly, use Potion if low HP, Flee if outmatched
5. Pick up items after combat — colored names indicate quality
6. Tap inventory icon (top-right) to manage gear
7. Find the green exit tile to go deeper

## Debug
`bun run debug:state` — print current game state to console

revisions.author: claude
